behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition." This depends on real git rename detection/status output and CLI subprocess behavior against an actual repo. A unit parser test can miss failures caused by real `git diff/status` rename semantics and thresholds. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent sequence are present in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, and run the CLI as a subprocess on disk state. Assert stdout report includes the file only in `renamed`, and `added`/`deleted` counts do not include that rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output." Binary diff handling is tied to real git output/exit behavior for binary content and real filesystem bytes. Mocked diff text can hide crash modes that occur only with real subprocess output and binary metadata.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, then run the CLI subprocess. Assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in counts without traceback.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "repository that has no commits yet" and "produces an empty report rather than an error." The unborn-HEAD case is a real git repository state and often surfaces through real git exit codes/stderr from subprocess calls; in-process fakes may not reproduce that boundary correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert zero-exit plus an empty structured report (not an exception/error payload).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed." This is an end-to-end correctness claim against real git history traversal and CLI execution, not just local counting logic. Mocking git history risks passing while real history parsing/revision-range behavior fails.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across several commits, run the CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert each category count exactly matches the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged." This explicitly depends on real git index vs working-tree states and how the CLI reads them via real repo/subprocess behavior. A fake index/worktree abstraction can miss real mode/path/rename edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
